message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  How do we forecast an emerging pandemic in real time in a purely data-driven manner? How to leverage rich heterogeneous data based on various signals such as mobility, testing, and/or disease exposure for forecasting? How to handle noisy data and generate uncertainties in the forecast? In this paper, we present D
                  <jats:sc>eep</jats:sc>
                  C
                  <jats:sc>ovid</jats:sc>
                  , an operational deep learning frame-work designed for real-time COVID-19 forecasting. D
                  <jats:sc>eep</jats:sc>
                  -C
                  <jats:sc>ovid</jats:sc>
                  works well with sparse data and can handle noisy heterogeneous data signals by propagating the uncertainty from the data in a principled manner resulting in meaningful uncertainties in the forecast. The deployed framework also consists of modules for both real-time and retrospective exploratory analysis to enable interpretation of the forecasts. Results from real-time predictions (featured on the CDC website and FiveThirtyEight.com) since April 2020 indicates that our approach is competitive among the methods in the COVID-19 Forecast Hub, especially for short-term predictions.
                </jats:p>
title: DeepCOVID: An Operational Deep Learning-driven Framework for Explainable Real-time COVID-19 Forecasting

reference.unstructured: Adhikari, B. , Xu, X. , Ramakrishnan, N. , and Prakash, B. A. 2019. Epideep: Exploiting embeddings for epidemic forecasting. In Proceedings of the 25th ACM SIGKDD, 577–586.
